gmw_00000010.name: M105
gmw_00000010.overview: M105 is a relaxed elliptical galaxy that dominates the nearby Leo I group [arXiv:astro-ph/0102374, arXiv:1406.6982]. It is a gas-poor system [arXiv:1206.2434] hosting an old stellar population [arXiv:0804.0319] and an uncertain LINER active galactic nucleus at its core [arXiv:1508.05065].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3379 with stellar, planetary-nebula, and satellite tracers, finding MOND can fit its velocity-dispersion data while baryons-only Newtonian dynamics fails outside.

quotes.quote: In this work, we solve the Jeans equation for the distribution of the velocity dispersion around elliptical galaxies, and in particular we fit the NGC3379 galaxy, where the most extended data is available for the velocities. We also further explore fits at larger radii for the special case of NGC 3379, with satellite galaxies as tracers, as done by KP07 and Angus et al (2007b).

NGC 3379 is modeled by a cored-Hernquist distribution (see Fig. logsigma ). The stellar mass of this galaxy is estimated to be M=1 10^ 11 M_ , assuming a constant mass-to-light ratio M/L (see Table massp ). In MOND (and Newton without DM), this stellar distribution is the only matter contributing to the gravitational potential.

MOND All scales are easy to fit with MOND. Stars: under 1 kpc, the gravitational potential is purely Newtonian, so MOND and Newton models are identical. The anisotropy needs to be a bit radial ( 0.2-0.4 ), but always far from the allowed limits. PN: until 5 kpc the potential is still Newtonian.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3379 as a declining-dispersion system where dark matter does not improve split-axis fits, though circular-ring data allow modest improvement for F_d about 7.

quotes.quote: NGC 3379 This galaxy is another of the notable systems that romanowsky2003 observed to have a radially declining _ los profile. For the coccato2009 data on two axes, the dark halo does not improve the fits over a S ersic-only model. Dark matter is allowed if the halo has a large radius and low central concentration. Cases with F_ d 9 are much worse fits than for lower F_ d .

For comparison with the split-axial results, we also fit the projected velocity dispersion data representing circular rings, which are extracted from figure 11 of douglas2007 who incorporated stellar data from statler1999 . A purely stellar model fits poorly: if the orbits are isotropic then ^2_r 4.1 . If dark matter is present in the cosmic fraction, then it improves the fitting somewhat: the best ^2_r 2 occurs for F_ d 7 (see lower middle panel, Figure fig.isotropic ). Isotropic models with F_ d 9 fit unacceptably worse than those with F_ d 8 .

sources.summary: The paper places NGC 3379 among low position-angle-difference ellipticals, finding its orientation poorly constrained and its bin-to-bin alignment results complex and not likelihood dominated.

quotes.quote: Galaxies NGC 3379, 4261 and 4551 are placed in this category. Plot of the posterior density of NGC 3379 in the entire range of ( ^ ' , ^ ' ) is presented in figure 12. Although it is not constrained (almost all value of ^ ' are in 1 regions), but the symmetry of the plots in pairs of octants is clearly seen.

Results of NGC 3379 are complex. The results of the locations of maximum probability and of the area of 68 highest probability of bins (1-3) are shown in table 5. We find that locations of maximum probability are not very close. Further, the orientation estimate of bin 1 is not very much likelihood dominated: 1 and 2 regions occupy 55.4 and 92.1 , respectively. Orientation of NGC 3379 is not well constrained even when the kinematical data is included (Statler, 2001, S94b).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3379 as the dominant Leo group galaxy with old central and resolved-star populations, modest hot gas, X-ray point-source contribution, and a poor bimodal GCS.

quotes.quote: NGC 3379 This galaxy is the dominant object of the nearby Leo group. TF2002 derive an old central age of 9.3 Gyr, consistent with ages larger than 8 Gyr determined through HST-NICMOS observations of resolved stars by GFMTC2004 . The galaxy contains a very modest hot gas halo, and RCSFJ2008 suggest that a significant contribution to the observed X-ray flux of the galaxy comes from unresolved point sources.

From a CCD study of the NGC 3379 GC system, AT94 detected 60 GCs but no evidence of bimodality. Using HST/WFPC2, LBHFG2001 confirmed that NGC 3379 contains a poor GCS. Both WFB2003 and RZ2004 found evidence of bimodality using broad band colours. These last authors estimated a total GC population of 270 clusters giving a low S _N value of 1.2 0.3. In their spectroscopic study, PBFBGFFZSHP2006 found the GCs to be consistent with old ages, i.e. 10 Gyr, with a wide range of metallicities.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3379 to test phase-space isotropy, finding its planetary-nebula and globular-cluster data support distinct phase-space densities and imply a dark-matter-rich elliptical galaxy.

quotes.quote: In particular, we configure the question of unequal estimates of gravitational mass density functions, using the available data sets, to the context of the real galaxy NGC 3379. For this system, multiple data sets are measured for two distinct types of galactic particles pns,bergond .

In terms of the phase space structure of this real galaxy NGC 3379, we can then conclude that the phase space of the system is marked by at least two distinct volumes, motions in which do not communicate with each other, leading to distinct orbital distributions being set up in these two volumes, which in turn manifests into distinct pdf s for these subspaces of the galactic phase space. The PNe and GC samples are drawn from such distinct pdf s. Of course, such an interpretation would hold up if we can rule out extraneous reasons that might be invoked to explain the differential support in D _1 and D _2 to the assumption of phase space isotropy. Such extraneous factors are systematically dealt with in Section sec:discussions . It merits mention that our result that (H_2 D _2) 1 also suggests that the phase space density that the observed GCs in this galaxy live in, is nearly isotropic.

NGC 3379 is advanced as a dark matter rich galaxy, with the gravitational mass inside a radius of about 20 kpc to be at least as high as about 4 to 10 10 ^ 11 M _ , where M _ denotes the mass of the Sun and the astronomical unit of length, kiloparsec, is abbreviated as kpc.

sources.summary: The paper studies M105 as a standard elliptical galaxy, using resolved RGB photometry to measure its distance, bimodal metallicity distribution, and dual metal-rich and metal-poor stellar halos.

quotes.quote: M105 is a standard elliptical galaxy, located in the Leo I Group. We present photometry of the resolved stars in its inner region at R 4 4R_ eff , obtained from F606W and F814W images in the Hubble Space Telescope archive. We combine this with photometry of the outer region at R 12 12R_ eff from archival imaging data. Color-magnitude diagrams of the resolved stars in the inner region show a prominent red giant branch (RGB) with a large color range, while those of the outer region show better a narrow blue RGB. The metallicity distribution function (MDF) of the RGB stars shows the existence of two distinct subpopulations: a dominant metal-rich population (with a peak at [ M/H ] 0.0 ) and a much weaker metal-poor population (with a peak at [ M/H ] 1.1 ).

These results provide strong evidence that there are two types of stellar halos in M105: a metal-poor (blue) and a metal-rich (red) halo. This is consistent with the prediction in the dual halo scenario based on the spatial distribution of the globular clusters in bright ETGs by par13 . Comparing the MDFs of the resolved stars in M105 with those of the resolved stars in faint to bright ETGs and in the intracluster field of the Virgo core, we suggested a dual halo mode scenario for the formation of massive ETGs.

sources.summary: The paper surveys PNe around M105 and reports an extended halo PN excess, radial changes in luminosity-specific PN number and luminosity-function slope, and evidence for intra-group light.

quotes.quote: M105 (NGC 3379) is an early-type galaxy in the Leo I group. The Leo I group is the nearest group that contains all main galaxy types and can thus be used as a benchmark to study the properties of the intra-group light (IGL) in low-mass groups. We present a photometric survey of planetary nebulae (PNe) in the extended halo of the galaxy to characterise its PN populations and investigate the presence of an extended PN population associated with the intra-group light.

We identify 226 PNe candidates within a limiting magnitude of m_ 5007, lim = 28.1 from our Subaru-SuprimeCam imaging, covering 67.6 kpc (23 effective radii) along the major axis of M105 and the halos of NGC 3384 and NGC 3398. We find an excess of PNe at large radii compared to the stellar surface brightness profile from broad-band surveys. This excess is related to a variation in the luminosity-specific PN number with radius. The parameter value of the extended halo is more than seven times higher than that of the inner halo. We also measure an increase in the slope of the PN luminosity function at fainter magnitudes with radius.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3379 as an elliptical with mostly oblate inner structure, weak triaxiality, and a decoupled nuclear dust/gas component likely of external origin.

quotes.quote: The intrinsic shape and orientation of the elliptical galaxy NGC 3379 are estimated by dynamical modeling. The maximal ignorance shape estimate, an average over the parameter space, is axisymmetric and oblate in the inner parts, with an outward triaxiality gradient. The 1 limits on total-mass triaxiality T are T<0.13 at 0.33 and T=0.08 0.07 at 3.5 from the center.

The available constraints on orientation rule out the possibility that the nuclear dust ring at R 1 5 is in a stable equilibrium in one of the galaxy's principal planes. The ring is thus a decoupled nuclear component not linked to the main body of the galaxy. It may be connected with ionized gas that extends to larger radii, since the projected gas rotation axis is near the minor axis of the ring. The gas and dust may both be part of a strongly warped disk; however, if caused by differential precession, the warp will wind up on itself in a few 10^ 7 years. The decoupling with the stellar component suggests that the gas has an external origin, but no obvious source is present.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3379 with stellar and planetary-nebula kinematics, finding a low dark-matter fraction while noting conflicting halo constraints from HI and globular clusters.

quotes.quote: For NGC 3379, we used spherical orbit models, with the constraints including stellar kinematics and 109 PN velocities. The best-fit models have an anisotropy parameter varying from 0.3 in the center to +0.5 in the outer parts. The cumulative M/L_B at 5 R_ eff is 7.6 0.9 , implying a DM fraction of 15--30 within this radius, which is surprisingly low.

There are other available constraints on the NGC 3379 halo. One is a HI-emitting gas ring in a near-Keplerian orbit at 100 kpc (Schneider 1985). The implied M/L_B is 27 5 , startlingly low compared to expectations of 150 for a group core (van den Bosch et al. 2003). We have also obtained 38 GC velocities to distances of 40 kpc (Bergond et al. 2006). These show a flat dispersion profile, indicating a more massive halo than the HI ring implies, and making a consensus solution between HI, PNe, and GCs difficult (see Fig. 3, left).

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 3379 stellar kinematics in detail, finding weak minor-axis rotation, a kinematic-axis twist, sharp transitions, and possible flattened triaxial or S0-like structure.

quotes.quote: We have measured the stellar kinematic profiles of NGC 3379 along four position angles, using absorption lines in spectra obtained with the Multiple Mirror Telescope. We derive a far more detailed description of the kinematic fields through the main body of the galaxy than could be obtained from previous work. Our data extend 90 from the center, at essentially seeing-limited resolution out to 17 . The derived mean velocities and dispersions have total errors (internal and systematic) better than 10 , and frequently better than 5 , out to 55 . We find very weak ( 3 ) rotation on the minor axis interior to 12 , and no detectable rotation above 6 from 12 to 50 or above 16 out to 90 (95 confidence limits). However, a Fourier reconstruction of the mean velocity field from all 4 sampled PAs does indicate a 5 twist of the kinematic major axis, in the direction opposite to the known isophotal twist.

There are sharp bends in the major-axis rotation curve, visible (though less pronounced) on the diagonal position angles, which closely resemble similar bends seen in the edge-on S0 NGC 3115. Moreover, there is an abrupt flattening of the dispersion profile, as well as local peaks in h_3 and h_4 , all apparently associated with the outer rotation curve bend near 17 , and all coinciding with a region where the surface photometry shows some of its largest departures from an ^ 1/4 law. The sharp kinematic transitions that we see in NGC 3379 are, as far as we know, unprecedented in any elliptical galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 3379 as an intermediate-luminosity E1 galaxy whose NMAGIC models fit photometry, kinematics, and PN data but retain a mass-anisotropy degeneracy.

quotes.quote: In dl09 a sequence of spherical and axisymmetric NMAGIC models fitting an extensive data set (photometry, long-slit and SAURON absorption-line kinematics, PN velocity dispersion data) was constructed to investigate the mass distribution and orbital structure of the intermediate luminosity E1 galaxy NGC 3379. No independent information on the inclination is available for this galaxy, and values of i>40 are consistent with the photometry. The effective radius R_ eff 47''=2.23 kpc at an assumed distance D=9.8 Mpc. The combined kinematic data set shows major axis rotation reaching 50 km/s at 20'' , with PNe indicating a further increase to 70 km/s at 220'' .

dl09 explored a sequence of spherical and axisymmetric models, together with some triaxial test models. They found that their results were insensitive to the adopted geometry. Both strongly radially anisotropic models embedded in massive dark matter halos and nearly isotropic systems dominated by the stellar mass are consistent with the data.

sources.summary: The review groups NGC 3379 with intermediate-luminosity ellipticals whose steep velocity-dispersion profiles were reanalyzed with PN kinematics, requiring dark matter despite remaining anisotropy uncertainty.

quotes.quote: Mendez+01 first showed that the steeply decreasing outer velocity dispersion profile of the intermediate-luminosity elliptical (ILE) galaxy NGC 4697 could be matched well with an isotropic Hernquist model. They thus concluded that no evidence for dark matter had been found out to 3R_e , but that dark matter could be present if the velocity distribution is anisotropic. Romanowsky+03 drew attention to three further ILEs with steeply decreasing _p(R) , NGC 821, 3379, and 4494, suggesting the presence of little if any dark matter in their halos. However, the well-known mass-anisotropy degeneracy Binney+Mamon82 is much stronger in galaxies such as these ILEs, with de Vaucouleurs type luminosity profiles and steeply decreasing _p profiles, than in galaxies with either more shallow luminosity profiles or radially constant projected velocity dispersions Gerhard93 . With NMAGIC particle models see deLorenzi+07 based on a variety of kinematic data, including several hundred PN line-of-sight velocities, for NGC 4697 deLorenzi+08 , for NGC 3379 deLorenzi+09 and for NGC 4494 Morganti+12 determined the range of quasi-isothermal halo potentials consistent with the data for these galaxies.

Fig. 1c shows the 70 confidence boundaries from the PN likelihood for NGC 3379 and 4697, and from a more comprehensive analysis for NGC 4494. While some spread in the allowed CVCs and anisotropies remains, as expected, dark matter is required in all 3 galaxies. Fig. 1c indicates that the most favoured CVCs may be slightly falling, as in the lower-mass systems in Wu+12 . Relative to the outer CVCs, the baryonic centers of these galaxies are quite centrally concentrated, as could be expected in gas-rich mergers.

sources.summary: The paper describes M105 as a relaxed elliptical with little gas or recent interaction evidence, whose photometry follows a smooth de Vaucouleurs profile to large radius.

quotes.quote: M105 (NGC 3379) is a classic elliptical galaxy, most famous for being a keystone of the de Vaucouleurs ^ 1/4 photometric profile deVau79 . Observations in HI and CO have revealed a dearth of atomic and molecular gas bhr92,sofue93,oosterloo10 , although there does exist a very small ( 150 M_ ) dusty disk in the inner 13 \ pastoriza00 . The kinematics of the galaxy's center show some peculiarities, leading to speculation about whether it is actually a face-on triaxial S0 galaxy statler99 , although kinematics gleaned from planetary nebulae show little evidence of rotation out to 330 \ ciardullo93, douglas07 . schweizer92 analyzed the galaxy for evidence of recent interaction and found none. In total, aside from the innermost regions, the galaxy appears to be in a very relaxed, pressure-dominated state.

Figure fig:m105 shows our photometry for this galaxy. It can be clearly seen that the ^ 1/4 model remains a good fit out to the extent of our data ( 850 , or 50 kpc), without significant variations within the uncertainty.

sources.summary: The dissertation compares M105 luminosity data with quasi-isothermal mass models, deriving scale and magnitude parameters and finding broad agreement outside the center.

quotes.quote: The comparison of the mass distribution of quasi-isothermal models with the luminosity distribution in galaxies M87 (NGC 4486, E0) and M105 (NGC 3379, E1) demonstrates quite high similarity. It is seen in Figs.  fig-a2.1 and fig-a2.2 , where M_p(R)/M for the limiting quasi-isothermal model (continuous line) and L(R)/L for both galaxies (dashed line) as functions of /r_0 are given ( L(R) is the integrated luminosity within the circle having radius R , and L is the total integrated luminosity). For M87 the integrated luminosity L(R) was found on the basis of the compilation of the surface brightness measurements in B-colour by deVaucouleurs:1978, deVaucouleurs:1979a . For M105 we used the results obtained by deVaucouleurs:1979b (also in B-colour).

For both galaxies the behaviour of the integrated luminosity L(R) is similar to the inner surface mass distribution of the model M_p(R) in very wide distance R interval. Only near to the very centre L(R) decreases with the decreasing R significantly more rapidly. But this discrepancy can be essentially removed when we choose for both galaxies q=0.975 ( a 40 ) instead of q=1 .

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 3379 as the brightest Leo I pair member, modeling its light profile, halo and intragroup-light transition, globular clusters, diffuse streams, and group-centered virial context.

quotes.quote: In this work we present new deep data of the LEO I pair, NGC 3379-NGC 3384, as part of the VEGAS sample. The new multi-band images represent the deepest data available for this target.

The LEO I pair NGC 3379-NGC 3384 The galaxies NGC 3379-NGC 3384 are members of the LEO I group. This is a very loose group composed, at least, of 11 bright galaxies where seven out of the total, including NGC 3379 (BGG) and NGC 3384, are associated with the subgroup NGC 3368 (M96) centered on the BGG.

Thanks to the large covered area (3.9 deg ^2 around the core of the pair) and the long integration time, we map the light distribution down to _g 30 mag/arcsec ^2 and out to 63 kpc from the BGG NGC 3379, and we provided a census of the GCs in this system. The main goal of this work was to derive the amount of IGL and its color, which are fundamental indicators of the formation history of the group. The main results of this work are summarised below. The new map of diffuse intra-group light extends out to 63 kpc and presents two very faint ( _g 28-29 mag/arcsec ^2 ) streams protruding from NGC 3379 and elongated toward North-West and South. The fraction of the diffuse light, coming from the stellar envelope plus the IGL, is 17 in both g and bands, with an average color of g-=0.75 0.04 mag.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3379 as a detailed low-dark-matter case study, comparing stellar, planetary-nebula, globular-cluster, HI, and X-ray constraints on its halo mass.

quotes.quote: A case study is NGC 3379, for which we have constructed dynamical models to fit long-slit stellar kinematical data in combination with PN velocities ; the models fully include the variations in orbital anisotropy which otherwise make the mass profile very uncertain. We find a total B band mass-to-light ratio at 5 R_ eff of _B=7.1 0.6 (solar units); for comparison, standard models of stellar populations imply _ *,B = 6--11, leaving very little room for DM inside 5 R_ eff . A major remaining systematic uncertainty in this analysis is the assumption that NGC 3379 is approximately spherical. But if it in fact contained a significant disk component (as in an S0 galaxy) viewed face-on, the large-radius stellar kinematics could appear ``cold'' even with a massive DM halo.

Although the galaxy's inner parts do not show the disk-like kinematics expected in such a scenario , a more convincing test is to use independent mass probes (Fig. comb1 , right ). The GCs are a good candidate since GC systems are rarely flattened; GC kinematics in NGC 3379 reveal a low halo velocity dispersion consistent with the PN result . A ring of HI gas at large radius also shows kinematics consistent with the low DM content .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3379 as an elliptical-galaxy rotation-curve test case, fitting extracted circular velocities and discussing its reported lack of dark matter.

quotes.quote: We have also included a fit to the elliptical galaxy NGC 3379 as shown in Fig. 3. The elliptical galaxy NGC 3379 has been the source of controversy recently . The velocities of elliptical galaxies are randomly distributed in the galaxy. However, the gravitational potential that would be experienced by a test particle star or planetary nebula in circular rotation about the center of the galaxy can be extracted from the line-of-sight velocity dispersion profiles. The data for R/R_ eff > 0.5 refer to planetary nebula.

We use the mean values of the extracted rotational velocities for NGC 3379, obtained by Romanowsky et al. and find that our predicted rotational velocities agree well with their data as shown in Fig. 3. According to Romanowsky et al. there appears to be a dearth of dark matter in the elliptical galaxy which needs to be explained by dark matter models and N-body cosmological simulations.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3379 as a bright E1 galaxy whose stellar and X-ray dynamical modeling finds no dominant dark matter within about 1.5 to 2 effective radii.

quotes.quote: NGC 3379 is a bright E1 galaxy (note the ellipticity 0.15 in Fig. ( fig:NGC3379_NGC4105_phot ) (left)); there are still some doubts whether this is a bona fide normal elliptical or a face-on lenticular galaxy (Statler 2001; Gregg et al. 2004 Sec 7), with a heliocentric radial velocity of 911 , (taken from the NED database) and absolute B magnitude -20.57. One arcsec in the galaxy corresponds to 63.12 pc. The effective radius is 54.8 arcsec (=3.46 kpc); see also the discussion in Introduction. Its kinematics have been the subject of several papers by Statler and collaborators (Statler 1994; Statler Smecker-Hane 1999; Statler 2001).

(iii) For NGC 3379 based on the 2I modelling procedures we find no evidence for dark matter which dominates inside 1.5 R_e and found that the constant mass--to--light ratio of this galaxy in the B -band for h_0=0.7 is between 5 and 9. This result is in agreement with previous studies of this galaxy (Ciardullo et al. 1993; Romanowsky et al. 2003). Analysis based on the X-rays leads to the same conclusion concerning the dark matter within 2R_e and the mass--to--light ratio of this galaxy if we adopt the parameters which are in agreement with those found in the literature: =0.64 (where is the slope used in the analytic King approximation model) and the temperature of the X-ray halo is between 0.26 (=1 T_ ) and 0.52(=2 T_ ) keV where T_ is the temperature based on the stellar velocity dispersion.

sources.summary: The review treats NGC 3379 as an ordinary elliptical with declining PN dispersion, GC kinematic contrasts, and orbit-model evidence allowing more halo dark matter through radial anisotropy.

quotes.quote: More remarkable behavior is seen in the five observed ordinary ellipticals, where _ p declines markedly with radius, with some galaxy-to-galaxy scatter in the slope (Fig. fig:fig2 , left). This suggests low DM content, as first inferred by from PN kinematics in NGC 3379. NGC 3379 is the only one of these galaxies so far with GC kinematics data (49 velocities; ; ; ). The GC halo dispersion declines much more gradually than the PNe, but the implication of this is not clear (see sec:halom ).

Having considered all these caveats , we turn to detailed modeling of NGC 3379, with extensive stellar kinematics ( ) and an initial data set of 100 PN velocities ( ). An ``orbit modeling'' method is used which constrains the anisotropy directly from the data, assuming spherical symmetry. The resulting models do indeed have radial anisotropy in the halo, allowing for more DM than suggested by Fig. fig:fig2 (left).

sources.summary: The review highlights NGC 3379 as a sparse-GCS, low-dark-matter elliptical whose GC, PN, stellar, and HI constraints yield differing but partly consistent mass-profile information.

quotes.quote: An example is NGC 3379, which is a very interesting galaxy but unfortunately has a sparse GCS. Several observing campaigns have achieved only 49 GC velocities , although another recent FLAMES study should double or triple this number (G. Bergond et al, in prep).

For the poster-child low-dark-matter galaxy, NGC 3379, there are not yet many GC velocities, but they do reach to much larger radii than the PNe. The GCS shows a much shallower decline in _ p (R) than the PNe (see Fig. romanow:fig:2 , left). The difference seems to be largely due to very different spatial and anisotropy distributions---e.g., GCs and PNe at the same projected radius are sampling very different regions of the 3D potential. The implied mass profiles from stellar, PN, and GCS dynamics are consistent within the still-considerable uncertainties. Intriguingly, this system also includes an HI gas ring whose kinematics imply a low halo mass that is not consistent with the GCS results.

sources.summary: The paper presents deep Chandra monitoring of the elliptical galaxy NGC 3379, cataloging its X-ray source population, luminosities, variability, globular-cluster matches, and luminosity function context.

quotes.quote: Trinchieri INAF-Osservatorio Astronomico di Brera, Via Brera 28, 20121 Milan, Italy Catalog of the X-ray sources in NGC 3379 Brassington We present the properties of the discrete X-ray sources detected in our monitoring program of the `typical' elliptical galaxy, NGC 3379, observed with Chandra ACIS-S in five separate pointings, resulting in a co-added exposure of 324-ks. From this deep observation, 132 sources have been detected within the region overlapped by all observations, 98 of which lie within the D_ 25 ellipse of the galaxy. These 132 sources range in \ from 6 10^ 35 \ (with 3 upper limit 4 10^ 36 ) to 2 10^ 39 , including one source with >1 10^ 39 , which has been classified as a ULX.

Source Variability Due to the monitoring approach that has been used when observing NGC 3379, both long-term and short-term variations have been able to be searched for in the galaxy's LMXB population.

The 10 GC-X-ray correlations that have been found in NGC 3379 are shown in Figure fig:gccorr , where an X-ray image with confirmed GCs is shown. The X-ray luminosity of the sources detected within NGC 3379 ranges from 6 10^ 35 \ (with 3 upper limit 4 10^ 36 ) up to 2 10^ 39 , where the brightest source, 70, is the only ULX detected within this system.

sources.summary: The paper presents deep Chandra monitoring of NGC 3379, describing it as a nearby isolated elliptical whose X-ray-source and LMXB populations probe old stellar-binary evolution.

quotes.quote: Presented here are the highlights from the deep \ observation of the elliptical galaxy NGC 3379. From the multi-epoch observation of this galaxy, 132 discrete X-ray sources have been detected within the region overlapped by all observations, 98 of which lie within the D _ 25 ellipse of the galaxy. Of these 132 sources, 71 exhibit long-term variability, indicating that they are accreting compact objects.

NGC 3379, in the nearby poor group Leo (D=10.6 Mpc) was chosen for this study because it is a relatively isolated unperturbed `typical' elliptical galaxy, with an old stellar population and a poor globular cluster system. These characteristics make NGC 3379 ideal for exploring the evolution of LMXB from primordial field binaries. NGC 3379 was observed by \ in five separate observations, carried out over a six year baseline, with the first of these, a 30-ks pointing, being performed in February 2001.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3379's kinematics to test its dark halo, finding viable fits with or without dark matter but allowing CDM-like halos if the outer orbits are radially anisotropic.

quotes.quote: Recent results from the Planetary Nebula Spectrograph (PN.S) survey have revealed a rapidly falling velocity dispersion profile in the nearby elliptical galaxy NGC 3379, casting doubts on whether this intermediate-luminosity galaxy has the kind of dark matter halo expected in CDM cosmology. We present a detailed dynamical study of this galaxy, combining ground based long-slit spectroscopy, integral-field data from the SAURON instrument, and PN.S data reaching to more than seven effective radii. We construct dynamical models with the flexible ^2 made-to-measure particle method implemented in the NMAGIC code.

We find that a variety of dynamical models both with and without dark matter produce viable fits to all the data. For assumed spherical symmetry we find that the data are consistent both with near-isotropic systems which are dominated by the stellar mass out to the last kinematic data points, and with models in massive halos whose outer parts are strongly radially anisotropic ( (7R_e) 0.8 ). In these latter models, the stellar mass distribution dominates in the center, and the dark matter fraction is 60 of the total at 7 R_e .

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 3379 as the first comparison galaxy in the monitoring program, noting its isolation, old stellar population, poor globular-cluster system, and detected X-ray sources.

quotes.quote: The first galaxy that was observed in this program was NGC 3379, in the nearby poor group Leo (D=10.6 Mpc; Tonry \ 2001). This system was chosen for this study because it is a relatively isolated unperturbed `typical' elliptical galaxy, with an old stellar population (age of 9.3 Gyr, Terlevich Forbes 2002) and a poor globular cluster system ( S_ GC =1.3 0.7 , Harris 1991; where S_ GC = No.GC 10^ (0.4(M_V+15)) ). From the 324-ks \ observation, reported in detail in Brassington \ (2008), 132 X-ray point sources were detected, 98 of which were found to lie within the D_ 25 ellipse of the galaxy.

Through HST observations of the galaxy the globular cluster system was identified in the central region and ten GC-LMXBs detected. These GC-LMXB associations were found to have high luminosities, with three sources exhibiting 1 10^ 38 . Also from studying this population it was determined that 48 of the detected LMXBs were found to show some type of long-term variability, with 5 of these sources being classified as transients and a further 3 of these as possible transients.

sources.summary: The paper applies its photometry-plus-dispersion formalism to NGC 3379, comparing the inferred mass density and circular velocity profiles with CHASSIS dynamical models.

quotes.quote: NGC 3379 NGC 3379 was reported by and to contain very little dark matter on the basis of a Jeans equation analysis of the kinematics of around 200 planetary nebulae (PNe) that reside in the dark halo of this galaxy. An independent estimation of the distribution of the total mass density of this galaxy was performed by Chakrabarty, 2009 (submitted to AJ ), by implementing these PNe velocities in the Bayesian algorithm CHASSIS saha_dal, dal_zwart . As acknowledged by Chakrabarty (2009), these mass distributions from CHASSIS indicate somewhat higher masses than the estimates of , owing to the assumption of isotropy within CHASSIS.

The resulting mass density distribution that we advance for NGC 3379 within 3 X_e , is depicted in black on the left of Figure fig:3379 . This is compared to the mass model identified by CHASSIS. v_c derived from our calculated mass distribution, from the dynamical mass modelling by CHASSIS and that reported by are represented in the right panel of Figure fig:3379 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3379 as a nearby giant elliptical benchmark for deeper resolved-stellar-population CMDs, noting its Leo-group dominance, distance, and central surface brightness.

quotes.quote: NGC 3379 is an Elliptical galaxy at a distance of 10.5 Mpc salaris98 which is 0.7 magnitude ((m-M) _0 30.3) less distant than Virgo ((m-M) _0 31), but it is a single system which may also not be very typical e.g.

Of course MICADO/MAORY will be able to make a more detailed CMD of closer by giant Elliptical galaxies, such as NGC 3379 (M 105) the dominant member of the Leo group, at a distance of 10.5 Mpc salaris98 , which corresponds to a distance modulus of, (m-M) _0 = 30.3 . This is thus 0.7 magnitude closer than the simulations at the distance of Virgo shown here. This means that the CMDs will go 0.7 magnitudes deeper in each filter for the same photometric errors, at the same surface brightness. The central surface brightness of NGC 3379 is about _V = 19 mag/arcsec ^2 . So we can look in the centre of this galaxy and go 0.7 mag deeper than the 19 mag/arcsec ^2 (and fainter surface brightness) CMDs shown in Fig. fig:cmd_elt_k in one hour of integration time.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3379 as a surface-photometry example, highlighting discrepant 2MASS profiles and showing that Sersic-based curve-of-growth estimates agree closely.

quotes.quote: An example is found in Figure 13, the surface brightness profiles of NGC 3379 from Jarrett and our study. The difference between the profiles is extreme at large radii, well beyond expectations from the RMS errors in the data. And the discrepant surface brightness profiles for NGC 3379 is not unique.

Figure 19 displays the results for all three techniques for the galaxy NGC 3379. The black symbols are the raw intensities summed from the image file. The blue symbols are the intensities determined from the surface photometry. The green symbols are the intensities determined from the fits to the surface photometric profile. Since the surface brightness profile of NGC 3379 is well fit by a S e rsic function, the curve of growth using the actual surface brightness data and the fit are nearly identical. Typically the raw intensities profile falls below the surface brightness intensities due to losses from masked pixels. This is the case for NGC 3379, the aperture values fall below the surface brightness intensities to produce a fainter total magnitude.

sources.summary: The paper concludes NGC3379 is effectively gas-poor in its analysis, with unresolved X-ray emission dominated by faint compact sources though low-density outflowing gas may remain undetected.

quotes.quote: The sample galaxies, NGC821, NGC3379, NGC4278, and NGC4697, have approximately identical K-band luminosities, and hence stellar masses, yet their X-ray appearance is strikingly different. We conclude that the unresolved emission in NGC821 and NGC3379 is built up from a multitude of faint compact objects, such as coronally active binaries and cataclysmic variables. Despite the non-detection of X-ray gas, these galaxies may host low density, and hence low luminosity, X-ray gas components, which undergo a Type Ia supernova (SN Ia) driven outflow.

The unresolved X-ray emission of NGC3379 was first investigated by david05 , based on a 32 ks Chandra observation. They concluded that X-ray gas may be present within the central 15 region with a temperature of kT=0.6 keV. Based on a much deeper, 341 ks, Chandra data set, revnivtsev08 claimed that NGC3379 is virtually gas free, and the unresolved X-ray emission is built up from faint undetected compact objects, mostly by ABs and CVs.

sources.summary: NGC 3379 has PNe velocity dispersions measured directly, is used for escape-speed illustration, and has MOND SMBH and mass-to-light results compared with dark-halo models.

quotes.quote: The PNe line-of-sight velocity distribution of NGC 3379 is shown in the upper panel of Figure fig:PNe . The velocity dispersion of NGC 3379 is obtained from the distribution by maximum likelihood method for 4 bins. This is consistent with the analysis by Gaussian Process Regression, see upper panel of Figure fig:PNe . We also obtain the velocity dispersion of NGC 4494 and NGC 821 by the same procedure. For NGC 5846, NGC 4374, NGC 1344 and NGC 4697 we take the values published in SAURON Coccato09 . For illustration we plot the escape speed of a test particle in NGC 3379.
From the table, we notice that the SMBH mass of NGC 3379 and NGC 4374 are consistent with the observed values McConnell11 .
In Table tab:masstolight , one notices that the mass-to-light ratios of NGC 3379, NGC 4374, NGC 1344 and NGC 4494 are too small for common dark matter model.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 3379 as an E0/1 elliptical whose planetary-nebula velocities show outer rotation, declining dispersion, little dark matter, and possible face-on S0 or weak triaxial structure.

quotes.quote: Fig. 1, due to N. Sambhus, shows the results of fitting the velocities of approximately 200 PNe from PN.S observations [ 15 ] in the E0/1 galaxy NGC 3379 with a non-parametric spline method. The velocity field shows a peak-to-peak rotation of 100 at 100'' , with a subsequent decline out to 300'' , confirmed by the rotation along the line of maximum gradient. However, both the detailed shape of the rotation curve and the reality of the apparent asymmetry in the velocity field remain uncertain.

PNe velocity dispersions in several intermediate luminosity ellipticals were found to decline with radius, and to require little if any dark matter at 2-5R_e [ 2,15 ] . Two of these (NGC 3379, 4494) were also part of the sample of [ 4,31 ] , but were consistent with constant M/L also in that study. NGC 3379 has been argued to be a face-on S0 and a weakly triaxial system viewed face-on [ 35 ] .

sources.summary: The paper presents PN.S observations of elliptical galaxy NGC 3379, deriving planetary-nebula kinematics, a declining velocity-dispersion profile, mass-to-light ratio, and a low dark-matter fraction within five effective radii.

quotes.quote: We present results from Planetary Nebula Spectrograph (PN.S) observations of the elliptical galaxy NGC 3379 and a description of the data reduction pipeline. We detected 214 planetary nebulae of which 191 are ascribed to NGC 3379, and 23 to the companion galaxy NGC 3384. Comparison with data from the literature show that the PN.S velocities have an internal error of 20 and a possible offset of similar magnitude.

We present the results of kinematic modelling and show that the PN kinematics are consistent with absorption-line data in the region where they overlap. The resulting combined kinematic data set, running from the center of NGC 3379 out to more than seven effective radii ( ), reveals a mean rotation velocity that is small compared to the random velocities, and a dispersion profile that declines rapidly with radius. From a series of Jeans dynamical models we find the B band mass-to-light ratio inside five \ to be 8 to 12 in solar units, and the dark matter fraction inside this radius to be less than 40 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 3379 with planetary nebulae, describing it as an E1 fast rotator with small rotation and an outer halo angular-momentum profile that remains nearly constant.

quotes.quote: Small rotation is observed in the E1 galaxy NGC 3379, while higher rotation velocities are observed in the S0 galaxy NGC 1023. The kinematics of the PNe system is in good agreement with the stellar absorption line kinematics in the radial region in which both data sets overlap, as shown in N+08 and D+07.

Both NGC 1023 and NGC 3379 are classified as fast rotators, they have a monotonically rising _R within 0.5R_e reaching 0.4 and 0.15 respectively. Their halos, however, behave differently. In NGC 3379, _R remains almost constant, ranging between 0.2 and 0.15; in NGC 1023, _R keeps rising up to 0.5 at R=2-3R_e and then declines monotonically, almost approaching the slow rotator regime.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3379 as weakly constrained by only radio and optical upper limits, with an ADAF model fitting the sparse SED but requiring high accretion.

quotes.quote: NGC 3379 This source has only two data points outside the X-ray band, one in the radio and the other in the optical, both of which correspond to upper limits. Therefore, there are few constraints for the accretion-jet model. The Bondi rate was estimated by david05 . Figure fig:seds03 shows that the AD model is able to reproduce the observed SED given the few constraints available, but there are no radio data to constrain the jet model in this case so we don't include it. The available data are not enough to constrain well the transition radius.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3379 as the main low-LMXB outlier, arguing its old colors and mass make IMF variation an unlikely explanation.

quotes.quote: Notable from Figure fig:xlf is that, at all luminosities, n_ x is remarkably similar between the different galaxies -- with the possible exception of NGC 3379, which has less sources, particularly at high . The reason for this single outlier remains uncertain. We note that the color of this galaxy is similar to the other galaxies in our sample and it is thought to have a similarly old age, suggesting the low n_ x is not a stellar population effect. Also, as discussed in Section sec:imf , the proposed variations in IMF with galaxy mass, should result in this galaxy having a larger, not smaller, number of LMXBs. A previously proposed explanation for NGC 3379's low n_ x is based on the only obvious difference between this and the other galaxies, its relatively low number of GCs.

sources.summary: The atlas classifies NGC 3379's ionized gas as Bubble morphology, with elongated inner H-alpha emission, a coincident dust lane, prior extended emission, and no obvious merger asymmetry.

quotes.quote: NGC 3379 (see Fig. Figure_AB2 ): We classified the ionised gas in this galaxy as Bubble , as we found evidence for extended H emission with an elongated shape in the innermost parts of the image ( 4 ). This substructure (PA 41 ) is spatially coincident with a nuclear dust lane at a PA 50 Masegosa2011 . also found extended emission when studying this galaxy. The H emission shows no particular asymmetric structures and no evidences of any previous merger interactions. When they apply a elliptical fitting to the brightness profile, their residuals show an X like shape, just as seen in our SD image (see Fig. Figure_AB2 ), usually present in barred systems.

sources.summary: The paper presents M105 as a Leo I benchmark where planetary nebulae connect a metal-poor halo to intragroup light and reveal core, halo, and envelope components.

quotes.quote: The results on the early-type galaxy M105 in the Leo I group represent a benchmark for the quantitative analysis of halo and intragroup light. Since the Leo I group lies at just a 10 Mpc distance, it is at the ideal location to compare results from resolved stellar populations with the homogeneous constraints over a much larger field of view from the PN populations. In M105, we have -- for the first time -- established a direct link between the presence of a metal-poor halo as traced by resolved red-giant branch stars and a PN population with a high specific frequency ( parameter).

By studying the PN smoothed velocity fields, LOS velocity dispersion (as shown in Fig. fig:M105-sigma ), rotation, and _R profiles, we separate the PNe associated to M105 into three kinematically distinct components: the rotating core covering the central 1 R_ eff of M105, with a metal-rich stellar population see, e.g. 2009mnras.398..561w and an in-situ origin, the inner halo, whose light distribution is described by a S e rsic profile, and composed of intermediate-metallicity stars that may either have formed in-situ or were brought in through few major mergers, and the exponential envelope beyond 7.5 R_ eff , with a metal-poor and PN-rich stellar population and high velocity dispersion, reaching that of dwarf galaxies in the Leo I group. The increase of the velocity dispersion profile at large radii, driven by PNe associated with the exponential envelope, may indicate that those stars are bound to the gravitational potential of the Leo I group at large and thus part of its IGL.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3379 as one of three intermediate-luminosity ellipticals whose PN.S planetary-nebula velocities reach 4--6 effective radii and show declining outer velocity dispersion inconsistent with simple dark-halo models.

quotes.quote: Observations with the PN.S on the 4.2-m William Herschel Telescope have allowed us to extend stellar kinematic studies to the outer parts of three intermediate-luminosity elliptical galaxies, NGC 821, NGC 3379, and NGC 4494 (Table 1). In each of these systems, we have measured 100 PN velocities with uncertainties of 20 km s ^ 1 out to radii of 4--6 R_ eff (Figs. 1--3). The line-of-sight velocities in the outer parts of all these galaxies show a clear decline in dispersion with radius (Fig. 4). A decrease in random velocities with radius has been indicated by small samples of PNe around NGC 3379 , but the more extensive data set presented here provides a more definitive measurement of this decline, and reveals that it also occurs in other similar galaxies. The new data are inconsistent with simple dark halo models (Fig. 4) and thus different from kinematical results for brighter ellipticals [ e.g., ] .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3379 as a nearly face-on early-type galaxy whose PNe kinematics may show a tilted major axis and a rapidly declining outer rotation curve.

quotes.quote: Preliminary results on PNe radial velocity fields of NGC 3379 and NGC 4494 from the Planetary Nebula Spectrograph, (PN.S, Douglas et al. 2002), have revealed that there is a tilt of the major kinematical axis of the PNe sample with respect to the stellar long-slit data as it is clearly shown in Fig. 1. Incidentally, the latter are viewed quite edge-on, while NGC 3379 and NGC 4494 are possibly nearly face-on. Furthermore, at least for the four systems mentioned above, the rotation velocity curves increase in the inner parts (up to 1 R_ e ) and rapidly drop in the outer parts down to zero at the outermost points.

For instance, with 6 VIMOS-IFU pointings it is possible to cover an area of 81 '' 54'' around NGC3379 center, corresponding to 1.2 R_ e 0.8 R_ e with an average S/N of 30 in a total of 14 hours integration. In this area a sample of at least 60 PNe is present, which is expected to be sufficient for any check of consistency between kinematical estimates (Napolitano et al. 2001).

sources.summary: The paper applies its one-component dynamical models to the round elliptical NGC 3379, reproducing its luminosity profile and inner kinematics while estimating M/L_B = 4.7.

quotes.quote: For the purpose, we picked the round elliptical galaxy NGC 3379 , which apparently does not possess significant amounts of dark matter ( , ; note that the models discussed in the present paper are one-component models and thus are not applicable to systems with prominent dark halos). This galaxy has an R^ 1/4 luminosity profile ( , ). For the kinematics, we considered the data of Statler Smecker-Hane (1999) and recently published data-points based on planetary nebulae that extend well beyond R_e ( ). The model that best describes the data is shown in Figs. fig:ngc3379ph fig:ngc3379kin . From such model, by adopting a distance of 11 Mpc for the galaxy and an absolute magnitude in B band M_B=-20.0 , we obtain a mass-to-light ratio M/L_B = 4.7 in solar units.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3379 as an early-type group or cluster host galaxy and finds its globular-cluster colours consistent with a broad single-peaked distribution.

quotes.quote: The host galaxies (NGC 1380, NGC 2434, NGC 3115, NGC 3379, NGC 3585, NGC 5846, and NGC 7192) were selected to sample a significant range in environmental density at intermediate galaxy luminosity and velocity dispersion in the range 19.2 M_B 21.2 and 184 264 km s ^ 1 , respectively. All galaxies are of early type ( T<-2 ) according to the RC3 galaxy catalog RC3 . Our sample includes five elliptical and two lenticular galaxies (see Tab. tab:galdat ).
The colour distributions of globular clusters in NGC 2434, 3379, 5846, and 7192 are consistent with a broad single-peak distribution.
Three galaxies of our sample (NGC 1380, NGC 3379, and NGC 5846) are assigned group/cluster membership, while the remaining four galaxies are considered field members.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3379 as Leo I's archetypal central elliptical, emphasizing conflicting planetary-nebula and globular-cluster constraints on whether its dark matter is sparse or extended.

quotes.quote: Leo I is the nearest (10 Mpc) example of a group containing multiple giant early-type galaxies, including the ``archetypal'' L^* elliptical at its center, NGC 3379. It is unclear if the group is reasonably relaxed, with a group halo centered on NGC 3379. This galaxy has been the focus of numerous dynamical studies, most recently employing PNe and GCs . The PNe imply surprisingly little DM inside 15 kpc, but leave the possibility that there are large amounts of DM spread further out in a massive, diffuse halo. The GC kinematics reach to 40 kpc, and do suggest a massive, even group-sized, halo (see Fig. romanow:fig:2 ).

sources.summary: The paper finds that unresolved soft X-ray emission in NGC 3379 follows an old stellar population and has nearly universal emissivity after bright LMXBs are removed.

quotes.quote: We use deep Chandra observations to measure the emissivity of the unresolved X-ray emission in the elliptical galaxy NGC 3379. After elimination of bright, low-mass X-ray binaries with luminosities 10^ 36 , we find that the remaining unresolved X-ray emission is characterized by an emissivity per unit stellar mass L_ x /M_ 8.2 10^ 27 \ M ^ 1 _ in the 0.5--2 keV energy band. This value is in good agreement with those previosuly determined for the dwarf elliptical galaxy M32, the bulge of the spiral galaxy M31 and the Milky Way, as well as with the integrated X-ray emissivity of cataclysmic variables and coronally active binaries in the Solar neighborhood. This strongly suggests that i) the bulk of the unresolved X-ray emission in NGC 3379 is produced by its old stellar population and ii) the old stellar populations in all galaxies can be characterized by a universal value of X-ray emissivity per unit stellar mass or per unit K band luminosity.

sources.summary: NGC 3379 is one of three elliptical galaxies whose long-slit and planetary-nebula kinematics are modeled with a modified potential, after prior dark-halo fits gave a decreasing dispersion profile.

quotes.quote: We will use the inner long slit data and the extended PN kinematics for three galaxies which have published dynamical analyses within DM halo framework: NGC 3379 ( 2007ApJ...664..257D ; 2009MNRAS.395...76D , DL+09 hereafter), NGC 4494 ( nap+09 , N+09), NGC 4374 (N+11). The decreasing velocity dispersion profiles of the first two galaxies have been modeled with an intermediate mass halo, M_ vir 12-12.2 M_ , with concentration c_ vir =6-8 and a fair amount of radial anisotropy in the outer regions.

We have found that the modified potentials allow to nicely model the three galaxies with a distribution of the parameters which turned out to be inconsistent with the results found in spiral systems. We have shown some hints that might be correlated with the galaxy anisotropy, , and the scale parameter, L , with elliptical and spiral galaxies following the same pattern.

sources.summary: The paper gives K-band decomposition notes for NGC3379, describing its morphology, fitted components, residual structures, and why the adopted model is preferred over simpler alternatives.

quotes.quote: NGC3379 (Fig. fig:NGC3379 ) appears to be a typical giant elliptical, with the brightness profile showing a core ( R<3 ) and no evidence of a disk. We thus fit it with a single- \ model while masking the core. The ellipticity is nearly constant at 0.1 .

sources.summary: The paper applies its inverse Bayesian method to NGC3379, inferring gravitational mass density from planetary-nebula and globular-cluster data and finding dataset-dependent mass-density estimates.

quotes.quote: In this section we present the gravitational mass density parameters and the state space pdf parameters learnt for the real galaxy NGC3379 using 2 data sets D _ PNe and D _ GC which respectively have sample size 164 pns and 29 bergond .

The method is used to learn the gravitational mass density of a simulated galaxy using synthetic data, as well as that in the real galaxy NGC3379, using data of 2 different kinds of galactic particles. The gravitational mass density vector estimated from the 2 independent data sets are found to be distinct.
It is also to be noted that the estimate for the gravitational mass density in the real galaxy NGC3379 appears to depend crucially on which data set is being implemented in the estimation exercise.

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 3379 kinematics, describing its rotation, velocity dispersion, halo behavior, and relevant photometric or interaction features in the early-type-galaxy survey.

quotes.quote: This is the largest galaxy in the Leo I group, classified as an E1. The PN system of this galaxy is has been widely studied in literature 1993ApJ...414..454C, 2003Sci...301.1696R, 2006AJ....131.2089S . The velocity maps from 2004MNRAS.352..721E reveal that the galaxy rotates regularly along its photometric major axis ( V_ rot ^ MAX 63 ), while the velocity dispersion has a central peak of 220 and it decreases to 180 around 0.25 . Then, the long slit data of 1999AJ....117..839S and 2009MNRAS.398..561W show that the profile gently declines to 120 at 2 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC3379 as one of the three brightest ELVES central galaxies, hosting 57 confirmed satellites and being excluded from the main MW-analog analysis as part of a higher-mass small-group subset.

quotes.quote: The three brightest central galaxies are NGC3379, NGC3627, and M81, which are also the first three in the K_s band magnitude-ranked figure legend. They host the largest number of confirmed satellites -- 57, 27, and 24, respectively. Interestingly, these three groups have the largest number of massive satellites and as mentioned in Carlsten:2022 , their group luminosity is significantly larger than that of the primary host. As explained below, we exclude these three `small groups' from the analysis as their higher mass, and likely different formation history from bonafide MW-analogs may bias the results.

sources.summary: The paper uses prior and current modeling to constrain NGC 3379 toward small triaxiality, supporting oblate axisymmetric interpretations of its distribution function.

quotes.quote: Statler ( Statler1994c 1994c) treats NGC 3379 using essentially the same data and methods applied here, except that the galaxy is fit in isolation, using a flat parent distribution. The result is that flattened nearly oblate shapes or rounder triaxial configurations are allowed by the data. Compared with this earlier result, the posterior density shown in Figure f.montage is more constrained toward small T due to the preference for near axisymmetry in the parent distribution.
Their use of oblate axisymmetric models for NGC 3379 and NGC 4472 is supported by our results for these galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC3379's BeppoSAX spectrum with a single power law; adding thermal plasma or an Fe line does not significantly improve the X-ray model.

quotes.quote: NGC3379 The single power-law spectrum provides an acceptable fit with ^2=21./16 degrees of freedom (dof); =1.65^ +0.44 _ 0.30 in agreement with previous results by Trinchieri et al. (2000). The addition of a RS component yields ^2   4 for two additional parameters (normalization and temperature) which does not represent a statistically significant improvement. The addition of a Gaussian line to the single power-law model yields only ^2 2.5 for two additional parameters. The 90 upper limit for the ew (equivalent width) of the line is 1.5 keV.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3379 orbit modeling to test elliptical-galaxy virial mass-to-light ratios, arguing its virial radius and mass-to-light ratio should exceed Romanowsky et al. values.

quotes.quote: Our lower bound on the mass-to-light ratio within the virial radius is taken from the work of , who performed, for the nearby giant elliptical, NGC 3379, an orbit modelling of the velocity field of the planetary nebulae combined with the radial profiles of the stellar surface brightness and line-of-sight velocity dispersion. Although they quote a mass-to-light ratio at 120 kpc, which they estimate as the virial radius, of _B = 33 14 , we find () = 2 _B G L_B H_0^2 ^3 = 507 \ , for = 120 kpc , _B=33 , and L_B = 1.51 10^ 10 L_ (from Romanowsky+03 's M_B = -20.0 and eq. [ MBsol ). So clearly the radius of overdensity 200 is much larger than 120 kpc, and since dark matter is believed to be more extended than luminous matter (e.g., Fig. figcomponents ), the mass-to-light ratio should be larger than 33. We extrapolate Romanowsky+03 's mass-to-light ratio out to the virial radius, as follows.

sources.summary: The paper reports a nuclear cluster of point sources in NGC 3379, identifies a radio-loud central LLAGN candidate, and interprets the brightest off-center source as an X-ray binary.

quotes.quote: NGC 3379 (T2, E1) harbors a cluster of 10 point sources in its nucleus. One of these point sources (not the brightest one) is at the exact center of the galaxy. The spectrum of this source had only 89 counts, thus its luminosity was estimated by fitting an absorbed power-law: L_ 0.5-10 keV =6.3 10^ 37 erg s ^ 1 . An unresolved radio source of S_ =0.7 mJy has been detected at 5 GHz at the center of this galaxy WH91 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3379 as a template early-type galaxy lacking warm emission, with a reported weak H feature constrained below earlier detections.

quotes.quote: This process was repeated with the emitters removed and reveal four more weak emitters, leaving three galaxies without emission line activity: NGC 1407, NGC 3115, NGC 3379. As an independent check of our template definition methodology, all seven of the potential template galaxies were subtracted from the galaxy with the strongest emission lines (NGC 2768) and we determined that the three template galaxies selected resulted in the high reported fluxes (i.e. lowest background). The three template galaxies, NGC 1407, NGC 3115 and NGC 3379, lack of warm emission is consistent with results from the narrow-band imaging detections for these galaxies.
NGC 3379 is reported to have a weak H in MFCF but an upper limit below this reported detection is determined in G94-5.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 3379's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 3379 is an elliptical galaxy at a distance of D= 10.6 1.3 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) with an active nucleus classified as a LINER/transition object ho_search_1997-1 . An unresolved radio core was detected in the nucleus wrobel_radio-continuum_1991 along with several X-ray point sources of comparable brightness flohic_central_2006 . The first MIR observations of NGC 3379 were performed with IRTF in 1983 impey_infrared_1986 .

sources.summary: The appendix reports that NGC 3379's chip gap was corrected, notes a possible central disk, and models the galaxy with Sersic plus outer disk-like components.

quotes.quote: NGC 3379 NGC 3379 had a chip gap across the nucleus, but we were able to correct for it. There appears to be a disk in the central 2" but the chip gap covers part of it. This galaxy is well fit by a S e rsic profile plus an outer disk-like component.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3379 as an uncertain LINER AGN whose nuclear X-ray source may be Compton-thick, with MIR-X-ray limits compatible with that scenario.

quotes.quote: NGC 3379 The uncertain LINER AGN in NGC 3379 shows several comparable X-ray point sources in the nuclear region, and identifies the most central but not brightest one as low-luminosity AGN with an observed X-ray luminosity of = 37.23 . estimate a higher X-ray luminosity ( = 38.1 ) from the same data but note that the low S/N makes reliable estimates difficult. Based on the low X-ray-- ratio, argue that the LLAGN in NGC 3379 is in fact Compton-thick obscured with = 39.91 . The upper limit on the intrinsic X-ray luminosity from the MIR--X-ray correlation would be compatible with this scenario ( = 40.04 ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3379 as M105, the dominant elliptical in the nearby Leo group, with low specific frequency and only 55 detected globular clusters showing uncertain bimodality.

quotes.quote: NGC 3379 Also known as M105, NGC 3379 is the dominant elliptical in the nearby Leo group. Along with NGC 3377, it has a relatively low specific frequency, i.e. S _N 1.2 ash98 . Here we detect only 55 GCs with bimodality likely, but not certain.

gmw_00000011.name: SMM J14011+0252
gmw_00000011.overview: SMM J14011+0252 is a gravitationally lensed starburst galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 2.5 [arXiv:1212.5955, arXiv:0907.1087]. The galaxy features warm molecular gas [arXiv:0907.1087], a starburst-like ultraviolet spectrum [arXiv:astro-ph/0203211], and no signs of an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0203211].

sources.summary: The paper maps CO(1-0) in the lensed high-redshift SMG SMM J14011+0252, constraining molecular gas conditions, orientation, and a star-formation relation unlike local normal galaxies.

quotes.quote: We present high-resolution CO(1--0) observations of the lensed submillimeter galaxy (SMG) SMM J14011+0252 at z=2.6 . Comparison to the previously-detected CO(3--2) line gives an intensity ratio of r_ 3,1 =0.97 0.16 in temperature units, larger than is typical for SMGs but within the range seen in the low- z ultraluminous infrared galaxy population. Combining our new data with previous mid- J CO observations, we perform a single-phase large velocity gradient (LVG) analysis to constrain the physical conditions of the molecular gas.

This conclusion is consistent with a scenario in which SMM J14011+0252 has a fairly face-on orientation and a molecular ISM composed of many unresolved clouds. Using previous H observations, we find that SMM J14011+0252 has a spatially resolved star formation rate vs. molecular gas surface density relation inconsistent with those of ``normal" local star-forming galaxies, even if we adopt a local ``disk-like" CO-to- H_2 conversion factor as motivated by our LVG analysis.

J14011 ( z_ opt =2.55 ; barger1999 ) was discovered as part of the SCUBA Lens Survey smail1998,smail2002 ; it consists of two optical/near-IR components in the source plane (J1 and J2) that are weakly gravitationally lensed by the cluster A1835 ( z=0.25 ) and one of its members J1c; motohara2005 , with a median magnification factor of 3.5 0.5 smail2005 .

sources.summary: The paper presents high-resolution VLT spectra of SMM J14011+0252, finding a starburst-like UV spectrum, a blueshifted interstellar line, LMC-like metallicity, and no H-profile sign of an AGN.

quotes.quote: In order to study two of the identified submm sources in more detail we have observed SMM J14011+0252 at z=2.565 and SMM J02399-0136 at z=2.808 with the VLT/FORS2 (longslit), with an observed spectral resolution of 1.06 .

For instance, the SMM J14011+0252 spectrum is typical of starbursts. We have compared the data with our models of star-forming galaxies STARBURST99 is available at http://www.stsci.edu/science/starburst99 . Fig. 1 (left panel) shows the spectrum of SMM J14011+0252 together with STARBURST99 model with the following input parameters: Star Formation Law = continuous, IMF = Salpeter and metallicity = LMC and solar, age=100 Myr. The dashed vertical line is centered on the interstellar line SiII 1526.7 which is blue shifted in SMM J14011+0252 (outflow velocity 290 km/s). The shape of CIV 1550 suggests that this object has metallicity similar to the LMC. The H profile in the VLT-ISAAC spectrum shows no sign of an AGN.

sources.summary: The paper detects SMM J14011 at 350 microns, notes prior CO and CI detections, and finds its warm dust inconsistent with a cirrus-only far-infrared model.

quotes.quote: SMM J14011 was the second SCUBA source to be detected in a molecular emission line, namely CO(3--2) at the Owens Valley Radio Observatory frayer99 . It has been intensively studied since then in CO lines see downes03 ; the CI(1--0) line has been detected weiss05 . The source is lensed, but the lens magnification is very uncertain and model dependent, ranging from = 5 - 25 downes03 .

In SMM J02399, our data are consistent with their model, but in SMM J14011, their model prediction is about a factor of 10 too low. This result is generally consistent with the \ values in Table 5: SMM J02399 has = 29 K, within the limit of 30 K that the dust achieves in their models; SMM J14011 has = 42 K.

sources.summary: The paper uses J14011 as a key submillimeter galaxy example, citing its borderline flux inclusion, measured nebular metallicity around 1.9 solar, and estimated 200 Myr age.

quotes.quote: We note that SMMJ14011 + 1152, with an intrinsic S_ 850 flux of 2.9 mJy, could be included in our sample if the magnification is slightly smaller. Our mean values do not change significantly if one includes SMM14011 + 1152.

In the case of J14011, TeczaM_04a used the near-infrared integral field spectrometer, SPIFFI (now SINFONI) on the ESO-VLT to measure the nebular emission line ratios of J14011. Using the classical optical diagnostic ratio, R _ 23 (= [ OII ] 3726, 3729 + [ OIII ] 4959, 5007/H ), TeczaM_04a inferred a metallicity of +0.27^ +0.11 _ 0.15 dex ( Z 1.9_ 0.5 ^ +0.5 Z_ ).
TeczaM_04a estimated an age for J14011 + 0252 of 200 Myrs from the strength of its Balmer break.

sources.summary: The paper describes SMM J14011+0252 as a strongly lensed z~2.5 starburst submillimeter galaxy with CO-traced warm gas, no detected water megamaser emission, and deep luminosity limits.

quotes.quote: Both SMM J14011+0252 and SMM J16359+6612 have multi-wavelength characteristics consistent with ongoing starburst activity, as well as CO line emission indicating the presence of warm molecular gas. Our observations do not reveal any evidence for H _2 O megamaser emission in either target, while the lensing allows us to obtain deep limits to the H _2 O line luminosities, L_ H_2O < 7470 L _ (3- ) in the case of SMM J14011+0252, and L_ H_2O < 1893 L _ for SMM J16359+6612, assuming linewidths of 80 km s ^ 1 .

In the case of SMM J14011+0252, the corrected 850 m flux density would imply, L_ FIR 6.7 10^ 12 L _ , for the same assumptions on the dust temperature. We should then expect an H _2 O megamaser luminosity, L_ H_2O 6700 L _ , which is also consistent with our observed limits.

sources.summary: The paper models SMM J140104+0252 as one of two lensed background submillimeter galaxies in the Abell 1835 field and estimates its 1.1 mm flux at about 6.5 mJy.

quotes.quote: The data is well described by a model including an extended Sunyaev-Zel'dovich (SZ) signal from the cluster gas plus emission from two bright background submm galaxies magnified by the gravitational lensing of the cluster. The best-fit values for the central Compton value for the cluster and the fluxes of the two main point sources in the field: SMM J140104+0252, and SMM J14009+0252 are found to be y_ 0 =(4.34 0.52 0.69) 10^ 4 , 6.5 2.0 0.7 mJy and 11.3 1.9 1.1 mJy, where the first error represents the statistical measurement error and the second error represents the estimated systematic error in the result.

A parameter search has been used to determine the best fit values of the central Compton parameter for the cluster and the fluxes for the point sources SMM J140104+0252 and SMM J14009+0252. These values are found to be y_ 0 =(4.68 0.48 0.82) 10^ 4 , 6.5 2.0 mJy and 11.3 1.9 mJy, respectively with a statistical signal-to-noise of 10.5, 3.3 and 6.0 respectively.

sources.summary: The paper measures Abell 1835a's faint H and bright Pa lines, compares literature extinction, and derives a high IR-recombination-line extinction of 5.9 mag.

quotes.quote: Abell 1835a shows a faint H line, too faint to measure asymmetry in the line profile, but a bright Pa line with a flux of 7.9 1.5 10 ^ 16 erg/s/cm ^2 . This is the second brightest Pa detection in our sample, after the 8 O'clock arc, whose Pa flux is 11.5 2.2 10 ^ 16 erg/s/cm ^2 .
Abell 1835a has extinction measurements by Nesvadba07 using H /H ratio of E(B-V) = 1.3-1.6 , implying \ of 4.0-5.0 mag.

From Table table_derivedqn , the extinction measurements from IR recombination lines for Abell 2218a, Abell 1835a, and the 8 O'clock arc, where we have secure IR line detections are 3.3 0.4 mag, 5.9 0.4 mag, and 2.8 0.3 mag, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper identifies SMM J14011+0252 as an early submillimeter galaxy detected in CO, argued to be strongly lensed, and observed in atomic carbon.

quotes.quote: SMM J14011+0252 SMM J14011+0252 is one of the first submillimeter galaxies (S _ 850 m =15 2 mJy, Ivison et al. \ 2000) that has been detected in CO emission (Frayer et al. \ 1999, J =3; Downes Solomon 2003, J =3, J =7). Downes Solomon argue that the source is strongly lensed, with a magnification factor =25 5. The \ line is shown in Figure 10 (see also Table 3).

sources.summary: The paper treats SMMJ14011+0252 as a lensed Lyman-break galaxy whose submillimeter and radio fluxes match far-infrared correlation predictions, allowing a UV-based star-formation-rate estimate.

quotes.quote: One example is the lensed Lyman-break galaxy SMMJ14011+0252 at z=2.565 . Its observed 850 m and fluxes of 15 2 mJy, 115 30 Jy agree well with the predictions of 23^ +14 _ 9 mJy, 150^ +95 _ 67 Jy that the /far-IR correlation implies . In this case the total star-formation rate can be deduced with reasonable accuracy from UV observations alone.

gmw_00000012.name: SMM J02399-0136
aliases_external: SMM J02399-0136 / L2SW far-IR starburst component
gmw_00000012.overview: SMM J02399-0136 is a lensed, high-redshift submillimeter galaxy at z=2.8 [arXiv:0912.1591, arXiv:1903.03131] that comprises a complex merger of multiple components [arXiv:0912.1591, arXiv:1805.07212]. The system is powered by roughly equal contributions from a highly obscured starburst and a broad-absorption-line quasar [arXiv:0912.1591, arXiv:astro-ph/0701816].

sources.summary: The paper presents SMM J02399 0136 as an archetypal z=2.8 SMG whose extended molecular gas, obscured starburst, BAL-QSO host, and faint component indicate a complex merger.

quotes.quote: We present new multi-wavelength observations of the first submillimetre-selected galaxy (SMG), SMM J02399 0136 at z=2.8 . These observations include mapping of the CO \ emission using elements of the Expanded Very Large Array, as well as high-resolution 1.4-GHz imaging and optical/infrared (IR) data from the Very Large Array, Hubble Space Telescope , Spitzer and Keck- i . Together these new data provide fundamental insights into the mass and distribution of stellar, gas and dust distribution within this archetypal SMG. The CO \ emission, with its minimal excitation and density requirements, traces the bulk of the metal-rich molecular gas, and reveals a molecular gas mass of 10 ^ 11 M _ , extending over approximately 5 arcsec ( 25 kpc in the source plane), although there is tentative evidence that it may be significantly larger.

Our data suggest that three or more distinct structures are encompassed by this molecular gas reservoir, including the broad-absorption-line (BAL) quasar from which the redshift of the SMG was initially determined. In particular, the new rest-frame near-IR observations identify a massive, obscured, starburst which is coincident with a previously known Ly cloud. This starburst dominates the far-IR emission from the system and requires a re-assessment of previous claims that the gas reservoir resides in a massive, extended disk around the BAL QSO. Instead it appears that SMM J02399 0136 comprises a merger between a far-IR-luminous, but highly obscured starburst, the BAL QSO host and a faint third component.

sources.summary: The paper identifies L2SW as the red near-IR component and likely obscured-starburst host, close to the radio emission and coincident with most CO flux.

quotes.quote: In what follows we will refer to four main components seen in Fig. model : L1 -- the BAL quasar to the west, the brightest UV emitter in the region; L1N -- the bright, compact source, 8 kpc north of L1; L2 -- the low-surface-brightness feature, 10 kpc east of L1; L2SW, the red feature seen in our NICMOS imaging extending 6 kpc to the south-east of L1. Radio continuum morphology In Fig. morphology (central panel) we see the 1.4-GHz synchrotron emission overlaid as green contours on the HST true-colour image, together with 50-k tapered CO contours. A knot of synchrotron emission is coincident with L1 but the majority of the radio emission extends to the east, close to the extended near-IR source, L2SW.

Alternatively, and more plausibly, the extended emission may be associated with a heavily dust-obscured starburst between L1 and L2. As we shall see in the next section, the host of this starburst, L2SW, is identified through IRAC and NICMOS rest-frame optical/near-IR emission, as well as wispy rest-frame UV emission structures representing leakage from patches of low obscuration (similar to the scenario proposed for SMM J14011 + 0252 by ivison01 ).

This is best illustrated by the amplitudes of the real and imaginary visibility components (Fig. uv ) which suggest that the CO emission is resolved on baselines longer than 10 k , with a total flux density of approximately 500 Jy. The position of the CO \ emission ( 02^h 39^m 51.96 0.^s03, -01^ 36' 59.8 0.5'' J2000; see Fig. morphology ) is consistent with the emission seen by SCUBA and PdBI. We cannot rule out a contribution to the CO \ flux from the quasar, but the majority of the emission appears to be coincident with L2SW.

sources.summary: The paper presents SMM J02399 0136 as a z=2.8 submillimeter galaxy merger with AGN and starburst components plus a newly identified molecular-gas component, W1.

quotes.quote: We present , , and observations using the Green Bank Telescope (GBT) and the Atacama Large Millimeter Array (ALMA) of the z=2.8 sub-millimeter galaxy SMM J02399 0136. This was the first submillimeter-selected galaxy discovered and remains an archetype of the class, comprising a merger of several massive and active components, including a quasar-luminosity AGN and a highly obscured, gas-rich starburst spread over a 25 kpc extent. The GBT line profile is comprised of two distinct velocity components separated by about 600 and suggests the presence of a new component of molecular gas that had not been previously identified. The observations with ALMA show that this new component, designated W1, is associated with a large extended structure stretching 13 kpc westward from the AGN. W1 is not detected in the ALMA data implying that this gas has much lower CO excitation than the central starburst regions which are bright in .

The molecular gas mass of W1 is about 30 of the total molecular gas mass in the system, depending on the CO--to--H _2 conversion factor. W1 is arguably a merger remnant; alternatively, it could be a massive molecular outflow associated with the AGN, or perhaps inflowing metal-enriched molecular gas fueling the ongoing activity.

sources.summary: The paper studies lensed SMM J02399--0136 as a luminous SMG with AGN evidence, SED-derived infrared luminosity, and an offset CO velocity measurement.

quotes.quote: Here, we follow this familiar pattern. We exploit the recent upgrade to the National Radio Astronomy Observatory's (NRAO's 1 ) Karl G. \ Jansky Very Large Array (VLA), which includes the provision of Ka-band receivers (26.5--40 GHz), to study the redshifted \ \ emission from three lensed SMGs: (i) SMM J14009+0252 lying at z=2.9344 ivison00, weiss09 , lensed by Abell 1835 and one of the brightest SMGs detected in the SCUBA Cluster Lens Survey SCLS; smail02 (ii) SMM J16359+6612, a bright SMG a z=2.5168 kneib04, kneib05 and lensed into three images by the Abell 2218 cluster, and (iii) SMM J02399--0136 at z=2.808 ivison98 , the first SCUBA galaxy and the brightest of the SCLS galaxies, lensed by Abell 370. We also search for \ \ emission in (iv) the Lyman-break galaxy (LBG) A 2218 284, located 22.1 arcsec from the centre of the lensing cluster in the same field as SMM J16359 ebbels96 . The optical spectrum of SMM J02399, coupled with its extreme luminosity and emission-line widths ( fwzi 1,000 km s ^ 1 ) have long been cited as evidence that this source contains a type-2 active galactic nucleus AGN; ivison98 .

For SMM J02399 we use the mid-IR spectrum of Lutz05 , with data covering _ obs 750 m taken from ivison98 . In Fig.  fig:seds we see the excellent agreement between the data and the best-fit SED template. The derived value of L_ IR = (80.7 8.1) 10^ 11 L _ is 30 per cent lower than the value reported in ivison98 .

SMM J02399 displays a significant offset in velocity between the measured \ emission and the \ spectrum of genzel03 , 100 , roughly twice that which could be ascribed to uncertainties in the Gaussian fits.

sources.summary: The review describes SMM J02399--0136 as an extended z=2.8 SCUBA galaxy with CO kinematics resembling a rotating disk and implying a very large dynamical mass.
